---
title: "plot_scenario_bands"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.





### plot_scenario_bands()

**Purpose:**  
Plots multiple forecast scenarios on one base R chart.

**What it does:**  
This function draws several scenario lines together so users can compare conservative, base, and optimistic outcomes visually.

**Use case:**  
Use this for financial planning when you want to compare possible future paths.

**Inputs:**  
- `scenario_df`: Data frame returned by `scenario_forest()`.
- `main`: Plot title.

**Output:**  
A base R multi-line scenario plot.

**Example:**  
`plot_scenario_bands(scenario_forest(1000, 20, c(0.04, 0.06, 0.08), 500))`
